title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Owner Authorization, Ethics, and Evidence · Type Recall
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs stakeholder outcomes, owner authorization, ethics, and evidence from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for authorized inclusive site-audit charter after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features.
Record who controls each target, permitted identities, paths, methods, times, data uses, and revocation contact before any automated request.
Separate RFC 9309 crawl preferences from contracts, copyright, privacy, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Stakeholder Decision · changed case · Review Stakeholder De…

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 5765 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate useful owner-authorized, privacy-minimized, inclusive crawl outcomes and claim layers; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate pure model, compiler, emitted Node, local HTTP, controlled site, browser, accessibility, security, load, and production claims. Artifact under review: authorized inclusive site-audit charter.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Evidence Layers, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate pure model, compiler, emitted Node, local HTTP, controlled site, browser, accessibility, security, load, and production claims.
